taxonomy: Cardamomum vitellinum is a species of flowering plant in the family Zingiberaceae. It is commonly known as yellow cardamom or Nepal cardamom. The plant is native to the Himalayas and parts of Southeast Asia. Cardamomum vitellinum is a perennial herb with aromatic seeds, similar to the more widely known green cardamom (Elettaria cardamomum). However, it is less widely cultivated and used compared to its green counterpart.
uses: Cardamomum vitellinum, also known as yellow cardamom, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its medicinal uses include treating digestive issues, such as nausea and indigestion, due to its carminative properties. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions like arthritis and asthma. In some cultures, it is used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, and to reduce fever. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of its medicinal uses.
